questions.title: Variable Declaration and Output
questions.description: Write a C program that declares an integer variable, assigns it a value, and prints it to the console.
questions.outputFormat: Print the integer N as it is.
questions.explanation: Declare an integer variable using int, read the input using scanf, and print it using printf.
hints: Use printf to output the value

questions.title: Sum of Two Numbers
questions.description: Write a C program that reads two integers and prints their sum.
questions.inputFormat: Two space-separated integers A and B.
questions.outputFormat: Print the sum of A and B.
questions.explanation: Read two integers using scanf, add them using the + operator, and print the result.
hints: Use scanf with %d %d to read two integers
Add the two variables and print

questions.title: Area of Rectangle
questions.description: Write a C program that calculates the area of a rectangle given its length and width. The area is length multiplied by width.
questions.inputFormat: Two space-separated integers L and W representing length and width.
questions.outputFormat: Print the area of the rectangle.
questions.explanation: Read length and width, multiply them to get area, and print the result. Handle potential integer overflow by using appropriate data type.
hints: Use int data type for the result

questions.title: Temperature Conversion
questions.description: Write a C program that converts temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit. The formula is: F = (C * 9/5) + 32. Output the result rounded to 2 decimal places.
questions.inputFormat: A single floating-point number C representing temperature in Celsius.
questions.outputFormat: Print the temperature in Fahrenheit rounded to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation: Use the formula F = (C * 9/5) + 32. Use float or double for decimal values and printf with %.2f for 2 decimal places.

questions.title: Simple Interest Calculator
questions.description: Write a C program to calculate simple interest. Given principal amount, rate of interest per annum, and time in years, calculate the simple interest using the formula: SI = (P * R * T) / 100. Also calculate the total amount (A = P + SI). Output both values rounded to 2 decimal places.
questions.inputFormat: Three space-separated values: P (principal), R (rate), T (time).
questions.outputFormat: Print the simple interest and total amount on separate lines, each rounded to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation: Calculate simple interest using SI = (P * R * T) / 100. Then calculate total amount as A = P + SI. Use double for precision and format output to 2 decimal places.
hints: Print each result on separate line
